#ActionWhy
#1Patch 175 fixable CRITICAL vulnerability/vulnerabilities immediately.Fixable CRITICALs are the highest-priority — a patch exists and you should apply it.
#2Evaluate 5 unfixable CRITICAL vulnerability/vulnerabilities.No patch exists yet. Consider switching to a different base image or adding compensating controls.
#3Fix CRITICAL Dockerfile issues (e.g. running as root, hardcoded secrets).Bad Dockerfile config makes existing CVEs far easier to exploit.
#4Reduce HIGH vulnerabilities (currently 1630) by updating base image.Switching to a minimal base like alpine or distroless eliminates most package-level CVEs.

📋 Dockerfile Best Practice Findings

CRITICAL: 5HIGH: 5MEDIUM: 5LOW: 1INFO: 1
SeverityRuleIssueLocationHow to Fix
CRITICAL [DF005] Hardcoded secret in ENV Secrets baked into ENV instructions are visible in the image layers forever. Line 7
ENV DB_PASSWORD=supersecret123		 Use Docker secrets or pass secrets at runtime: docker run -e SECRET=$SECRET
CRITICAL [DF005] Hardcoded secret in ENV Secrets baked into ENV instructions are visible in the image layers forever. Line 8
ENV API_KEY=sk-abc123xyz		 Use Docker secrets or pass secrets at runtime: docker run -e SECRET=$SECRET
CRITICAL [DF005] Hardcoded secret in ENV Secrets baked into ENV instructions are visible in the image layers forever. Line 9
ENV MY_TOKEN=abc123		 Use Docker secrets or pass secrets at runtime: docker run -e SECRET=$SECRET
CRITICAL [DF007] curl | bash anti-pattern Piping a remote script directly to a shell is a major supply-chain risk. Line 17
RUN curl https://get.someservice.io/install.sh | bash		 Download the script first, verify its checksum, then execute it.
CRITICAL [DF014] AWS Access Key detected AWS key hardcoded in Dockerfile — rotate it immediately. Line 40
AWS API KEY = "AKIA1234567890ABCDEF"		 Use IAM roles or pass via environment variables at runtime.
HIGH [DF001] Avoid :latest tag Using ':latest' is unpredictable — the image changes silently over time. Line 4
FROM ubuntu:latest		 Pin to a specific version, e.g. FROM nginx:1.25.3
HIGH [DF008] sudo used in RUN Using sudo inside a container is unnecessary and increases attack surface. Line 20
RUN sudo chmod 777 /app		 Use USER root sparingly or switch users properly.
HIGH [DF013] Insecure tool installed Telnet sends data in plain text — never install it in a container. Line 30
RUN apt-get install -y telnet		 Use SSH or encrypted alternatives instead.
HIGH [DF014] Insecure tool installed FTP sends data in plain text — never install it in a container. Line 33
RUN apt-get install -y ftp		 Use SFTP or encrypted alternatives instead.
HIGH [DF004] No USER instruction found No USER instruction detected — container will run as root by default. —
—		 Add USER <non-root-user> before the CMD/ENTRYPOINT instruction.
MEDIUM [DF009] chmod 777 detected World-writable permissions allow any process to modify critical files. Line 20
RUN sudo chmod 777 /app		 Use the minimum permissions needed, e.g. chmod 755
MEDIUM [DF010] Package cache not cleaned Leaving apt cache bloats the image and increases the attack surface. Line 14
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl python3		 Add && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* at the end of your apt-get install line.
MEDIUM [DF010] Package cache not cleaned Leaving apt cache bloats the image and increases the attack surface. Line 30
RUN apt-get install -y telnet		 Add && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* at the end of your apt-get install line.
MEDIUM [DF010] Package cache not cleaned Leaving apt cache bloats the image and increases the attack surface. Line 33
RUN apt-get install -y ftp		 Add && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* at the end of your apt-get install line.
MEDIUM [DF015] SSL verification skipped in package installation Skipping SSL verification allows MITM attacks when downloading packages. Line 36
wget.*--no-check-certificate		 Remove --no-check-certificate and ensure proper CA certificates are installed.
LOW [DF011] ADD used instead of COPY ADD has implicit behaviors (auto-extraction). COPY is safer and more explicit. Line 23
ADD ./myapp /app		 Replace ADD with COPY unless you specifically need URL fetching or auto-extraction.
INFO [DF012] No HEALTHCHECK instruction Without a HEALTHCHECK, Docker cannot automatically detect if your app is broken. —
—		 Add: HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -f http://localhost/ || exit 1
